core_payload.content:
# Planner

## 1. Role
Planner is the strategic and tactical engine of the N1Hub Workspace. It now routes that planning field through four bounded sub-hubs so planning objects, horizon compression, operator surfaces, and signal intelligence remain explicit instead of collapsing into one 19-link parent hub.

## 2. Routing Surfaces
- `planning-objects` owns projects, goals, milestones, tasks, and roadmaps as the explicit planning artifacts whose shape the planner governs.
- `horizon-compression` owns Planning Horizon Engine plus daily and roadmap planning agents that compress long-range intent into near-term action.
- `operator-surfaces` owns the assistant and visible planning surfaces that explain, project, and schedule the plan back to the user.
- `signal-intelligence` owns tracker, swarm, branch, librarian, and agent-lane signals that pressure the planner to re-sequence work under real execution conditions.

## 3. Parent Boundary
The parent Planner hub keeps only the top-level relationship to Workspace, CapsuleOS, To Dig Deep, and the four planning sub-hubs. Detailed planning routing now flows through those sub-hubs so Planner remains the sovereign planning contract for N1Hub instead of a flat registry of every object, horizon, surface, and signal lane.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Planner now acts as a bounded routing hub for four explicit planning surfaces: planning objects, horizon compression, operator surfaces, and signal intelligence. The parent keeps only top-level ties to Workspace, CapsuleOS, To Dig Deep, and the four sub-hubs. This makes planning easier to inspect because work artifacts, multi-horizon compression, user-facing planning surfaces, and execution-pressure signals no longer hide inside one overloaded parent capsule. The result is a clearer planning contract for how N1Hub structures intent, compresses it into action, and revises it under real execution pressure.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Refactored Planner into four bounded sub-hubs so planning objects, horizon compression, operator surfaces, and signal intelligence no longer collapse into a 19-link parent hub.
